title: Migration v6: non-atomic ALTER TABLE — interrupted migration corrupts DB permanently
description: Trigger: process killed between the 1st and 4th ALTER TABLE in the v6 migration block (e.g., OOM kill during wallet init). PRAGMA user_version = 6 has not run yet, so on next open version < 6 is still true. The already-added columns cause 'duplicate column name: note_diversifier' on the first ALTER TABLE re-attempt → WalletStore::new() returns Err → wallet permanently unusable without manual sqlite3 surgery. Earlier migrations used CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (idempotent). ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN has no IF NOT EXISTS clause before SQLite 3.37.0. Fix: wrap the four ALTER TABLEs and the PRAGMA in a single explicit sqlx transaction — SQLite DDL is transactional.
close_reason: wrapped 4 ALTER TABLEs + PRAGMA in a single sqlx transaction; SQLite DDL is transactional, so mid-migration kill now rolls back cleanly
